segments.text: Big news for Indians wanting to work in the US. A new policy by the Trump administration is impacting the US H1B visa, a crucial route for many Indian
professionals. The US has introduced a staggering $100,000 fee for all new H1B petitions, effective immediately. This
controversial move drastically increases the cost for companies to hire foreign talent. While existing H1B holders and visa renewals are unaffected, this new
move will profoundly impact new applicants from India who are the largest group of H1B recipients. The goal is to protect domestic jobs and
ensure only the highest skilled workers are hired. But critics argue this could hinder innovation as many US companies depend heavily on talent from India.
However, this could be a silver lining for India. By reducing opportunities in the US, this policy may significantly reduce brain drain. This means more of
our brightest minds are likely to build their careers here at home, boosting India's own innovation ecosystem and fast growing tech sector. Subscribe to
